lines.line: # Introduction to the Oral History Project

> The speaker, Kate Cole, introduces herself as an art major at the College of Idaho and outlines the collaborative project she is working on with the Idaho Historical Society, supported by the NEA's Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program. The purpose of the project is to conduct an oral history interview with Lance Richard McGrath, an academic librarian and Education Technologist at the College of Idaho, to reflect on his personal and professional experiences in Idaho's Treasure Valley.

metadata.title: Introduction to the Oral History Project
metadata.synopsis: The speaker, Kate Cole, introduces herself as an art major at the College of Idaho and outlines the collaborative project she is working on with the Idaho Historical Society, supported by the NEA's Spotlight on the Humanities Grant program. The purpose of the project is to conduct an oral history interview with Lance Richard McGrath, an academic librarian and Education Technologist at the College of Idaho, to reflect on his personal and professional experiences in Idaho's Treasure Valley.

lines.line: # Childhood Memories on an Idaho Farm

> The speaker reminisces about their early childhood on a family farm in Eagle, Idaho. They share vivid memories of playing in the cornfields, helping their father hunt pheasants, and the mischief of throwing dirt clods. The speaker also recalls the simple joys of gardening, playing in a sandbox made from a tractor tire, and the experience of flood irrigation water. These early experiences fostered a deep connection to Idaho and an appreciation for agriculture and the outdoors.

metadata.title: Childhood Memories on an Idaho Farm
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reminisces about their early childhood on a family farm in Eagle, Idaho. They share vivid memories of playing in the cornfields, helping their father hunt pheasants, and the mischief of throwing dirt clods. The speaker also recalls the simple joys of gardening, playing in a sandbox made from a tractor tire, and the experience of flood irrigation water. These early experiences fostered a deep connection to Idaho and an appreciation for agriculture and the outdoors.

lines.line: # Family Background and Moving Away

> The speaker discusses their family's move from Idaho during their early childhood, which led to them starting kindergarten in Oregon. They describe their father's work as a moving truck driver and their mother's role as a homemaker. Despite moving around the Pacific Northwest, the speaker maintained strong family ties in Idaho, which influenced their decision to return for college education.

metadata.title: Family Background and Moving Away
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses their family's move from Idaho during their early childhood, which led to them starting kindergarten in Oregon. They describe their father's work as a moving truck driver and their mother's role as a homemaker. Despite moving around the Pacific Northwest, the speaker maintained strong family ties in Idaho, which influenced their decision to return for college education.

lines.line: # Journey to Becoming a Librarian

> Despite initially having a fear of librarians and struggling with citing sources, the speaker's career path eventually led them to librarianship. They reflect on their time as a philosophy major at Northwest Nazarene College and their work experience at Micron Electronics, which helped them realize their role as a 'reference librarian' for their team. This realization prompted them to pursue a Master of Library Information Science, which they completed in 2006.

metadata.title: Journey to Becoming a Librarian
metadata.synopsis: Despite initially having a fear of librarians and struggling with citing sources, the speaker's career path eventually led them to librarianship. They reflect on their time as a philosophy major at Northwest Nazarene College and their work experience at Micron Electronics, which helped them realize their role as a 'reference librarian' for their team. This realization prompted them to pursue a Master of Library Information Science, which they completed in 2006.

lines.line: # Service on the Nampa Planning and Zoning Commission

> The speaker details their involvement with the Nampa Planning and Zoning Commission, starting as a community activist and eventually becoming the chair. They faced challenges during the recession, dealing with stalled development projects and efforts to control urban sprawl. The speaker emphasizes the importance of balancing community needs with development and preserving the unique qualities of the area.

metadata.title: Service on the Nampa Planning and Zoning Commission
metadata.synopsis: The speaker details their involvement with the Nampa Planning and Zoning Commission, starting as a community activist and eventually becoming the chair. They faced challenges during the recession, dealing with stalled development projects and efforts to control urban sprawl. The speaker emphasizes the importance of balancing community needs with development and preserving the unique qualities of the area.

lines.line: # Contributions to the College of Idaho

> The speaker recounts their transition to the College of Idaho as a user services librarian and their involvement in significant projects, including the planning of the Cruiser Murray Library and the migration of the library's collection to a cloud-based system. They also took on the role of educational technology coordinator, leveraging their degree in educational technology to support faculty and students during the transition to online learning due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

metadata.title: Contributions to the College of Idaho
metadata.synopsis: The speaker recounts their transition to the College of Idaho as a user services librarian and their involvement in significant projects, including the planning of the Cruiser Murray Library and the migration of the library's collection to a cloud-based system. They also took on the role of educational technology coordinator, leveraging their degree in educational technology to support faculty and students during the transition to online learning due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

lines.line: # Focus on Educational Technology and Accessibility

> The speaker discusses their goals and interests related to their degree in educational technology from Boise State University. They explain how technology can be used to enhance learning and teaching, and how they have supported both faculty and students in using various technologies. The speaker also mentions their collaboration with the Department of Accessibility to ensure that educational materials and technologies are accessible to all students.

metadata.title: Focus on Educational Technology and Accessibility
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses their goals and interests related to their degree in educational technology from Boise State University. They explain how technology can be used to enhance learning and teaching, and how they have supported both faculty and students in using various technologies. The speaker also mentions their collaboration with the Department of Accessibility to ensure that educational materials and technologies are accessible to all students.

lines.line: # Pursuing a PhD in Educational Leadership

> The speaker is currently a PhD candidate in educational leadership, focusing on high school teachers' perceptions of their students' information literacy and collaboration with school librarians. They have completed their coursework and dissertation and are preparing to defend their dissertation. The speaker's academic journey reflects their commitment to education and library science.

metadata.title: Pursuing a PhD in Educational Leadership
metadata.synopsis: The speaker is currently a PhD candidate in educational leadership, focusing on high school teachers' perceptions of their students' information literacy and collaboration with school librarians. They have completed their coursework and dissertation and are preparing to defend their dissertation. The speaker's academic journey reflects their commitment to education and library science.

lines.line: # Leading the Idaho Library Association

> As the president of the Idaho Library Association, the speaker is actively involved in library advocacy, particularly against state-sponsored censorship and restrictions on library materials. They discuss the legislative challenges faced by libraries in Idaho and the importance of upholding First Amendment rights and access to information. The speaker's role involves informing librarians and the public about these issues and building coalitions to protect library freedoms.

metadata.title: Leading the Idaho Library Association

They discuss the legislative challenges faced by libraries in Idaho and the importance of upholding First Amendment rights and access to information. The speaker's role involves informing librarians and the public about these issues and building coalitions to protect library freedoms.

lines.line: # Supporting Public Libraries in Idaho

> The speaker has played a significant role in supporting public libraries in Idaho, including the Nampa Public Library. They helped form the Nampa Public Library Foundation, which raised funds for a new library and continues to support library programs and services. The speaker proudly mentions the foundation's success in bringing the first bookmobile to Nampa, which extends library services to those who cannot easily access the physical library.

metadata.title: Supporting Public Libraries in Idaho

lines.line: # Influential Role Models and Mentors

> The speaker reflects on the various individuals who have served as role models and mentors throughout their life. They express gratitude for the lessons learned from their grandparents and parents, as well as influential figures such as Governor Cecil Andrus and professors from their college years. These individuals have shaped the speaker's values, work ethic, and intellectual growth.

metadata.title: Influential Role Models and Mentors
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on the various individuals who have served as role models and mentors throughout their life. They express gratitude for the lessons learned from their grandparents and parents, as well as influential figures such as Governor Cecil Andrus and professors from their college years. These individuals have shaped the speaker's values, work ethic, and intellectual growth.

lines.line: # A Deep Connection to Idaho

> The speaker expresses a profound sense of belonging to Idaho, not just the Treasure Valley, but the state as a whole. They feel a responsibility to communities and individuals across Idaho, especially in their role with the Idaho Library Association. The speaker's connection to Idaho is rooted in their life experiences and the values they have cultivated over the years.

story.title: McGrath_Lance_OH2815 Hist305
story.description: The transcript outlines an oral history interview with Lance Richard McGrath, an academic librarian at the College of Idaho. Key topics include McGrath's upbringing in Idaho, emphasizing his roots in a farming community and the cultural and environmental influences that shaped him. The discussion covers his career path, detailing his evolution from philosophy student to librarian and his involvement in educational technology. McGrath has been active in local development as a commissioner and advocate on the Nampa Planning and Zoning Commission and has played significant roles in the development of library services, both in Idaho and in educational tech. Moreover, his commitment to library advocacy at the state level, notably against censorship, highlights his dedication to information freedom and community service. His roles at the College of Idaho and within the Idaho Library Association underscore his focus on equity, access, and education in Idaho.
